quarter: FQ3 2026 (fiscal third quarter, quarter ended March 31, 2026)

keyMetrics.m365CopilotPaidSeats: 20M+ (up ~33% sequentially from ~15M in Jan 2026; seat adds +250% YoY)
keyMetrics.capexOutlook: ~$40B in the current quarter; ~$190B for calendar 2026 (~$25B of the increase attributed to memory/component price inflation)

aiCommentary: AI was the dominant theme. Microsoft's AI annual revenue run-rate surpassed $37B (up ~123% YoY), and Azure's reacceleration to 40% was driven substantially by AI inference and training demand. Management highlighted M365 Copilot crossing 20M paid seats and framed the ~$190B calendar-2026 capex as required to meet AI demand, while acknowledging meaningful memory/component cost inflation. The April 27 OpenAI restructuring (removing Azure exclusivity and IP revenue-share, extending IP licensing to 2032) was presented as margin-accretive over time, and management is moving toward more consumption-based AI pricing. Separately, the MAI Superintelligence Team's in-house models (launched at Build 2026) underscore a strategy of long-term AI self-sufficiency.
demandCommentary: Demand commentary was broadly positive: Azure consumption growth reaccelerated and was described as constrained more by capacity/supply than by demand, supporting the heavy capex. Microsoft Cloud crossing $50B and Productivity & Business Processes growing 17% pointed to durable enterprise spend, while More Personal Computing was roughly flat (-1%), reflecting a soft PC/legacy backdrop relative to surging cloud. Management guided next-quarter Azure to 39-40% constant-currency growth, signaling confidence that AI-driven demand remains strong into FQ4 2026.

keyQuotes.quote: Cloud and AI are the essential inputs to every business' expansion. We're innovating across the tech stack to help customers adapt and grow in this new era.
keyQuotes.speaker: Satya Nadella, Chairman and CEO

keyQuotes.quote: Our AI business has now surpassed an annual revenue run rate of $37 billion, up 123% year over year.
keyQuotes.speaker: Amy Hood, EVP and CFO

keyQuotes.quote: Capital expenditures for the year will reach roughly $190 billion, with a significant portion of the increase driven by memory and component price inflation rather than additional capacity.
keyQuotes.speaker: Amy Hood, EVP and CFO (paraphrased from earnings commentary)
